summary: Website for listing hostels with separate categories for girls, boys, and co-living spaces. Features include hostel images, maps, room sharing details, pricing information, and a clean homepage.
architectural_notes: Website type project with hostel listing functionality
Need image hosting/storage for hostel photos
Map integration required (Google Maps or similar)
Sample hostel data includes Hyderabad location with various sharing options
Manual hostel entry form required with fields for all hostel attributes
Google Maps integration needed for location selection during hostel creation
Edit hostel functionality required for updating room sharing options and pricing
Privacy policy and disclaimer pages required for legal compliance
SEO meta tags needed for Google indexing (title, description, keywords, Open Graph, sitemap)
known_issues: Site not indexed by Google yet - SEO meta tags and Google Search Console submission needed
Site still not visible in Google search results - requires additional SEO improvements beyond meta tags (structured data, backlinks, content optimization)
Category filtering (All Hostels, Girls Only, Boys Only, Co-Living) not working on the home page

existing_features.title: Design and implement a clean, modern homepage that displays hostel categories (Girls, Boys, Co-Living) with category selection or filtering
Create hostel listing cards that display hostel images, name, category badge, price, room sharing details, and location information
Integrate interactive maps that show hostel locations with markers for each listed hostel
Implement a detailed hostel view page that shows all images in a gallery, complete room details, pricing breakdown, map location, and sharing arrangements

Apply a cohesive visual design with a welcoming, hospitality-focused aesthetic using warm, inviting colors (earth tones, soft oranges, or greens), clean typography, and spacious layouts that emphasize comfort and community
Render generated hostel images as static assets in frontend/public/assets/generated and display them in hostel listings and detail views
Display the newly added Hyderabad hostels on the homepage with proper category filtering, showing all hostel details including sharing capacity and pricing information in the hostel cards
Display the hostel owner's contact number on hostel listing cards in the HostelCard component, shown below the address with a phone icon
Display the hostel owner's contact number prominently on the HostelDetail page, positioned near the hostel name and address in a dedicated contact section
Add a search bar component to the Home page that filters hostels by name or address in real-time as the user types
Add advanced filter controls to the Home page for price range and room sharing capacity that work alongside the existing category filter
Add sort controls to the Home page that allow users to sort hostel listings by price (low to high, high to low), name (A-Z, Z-A), or location

Create an AddHostel page component with a multi-step form that collects: hostel name, category selection (Girls/Boys/Co-Living), description, full address, owner contact number, and room sharing options with individual price inputs for each sharing type
Integrate Google Maps location picker in the AddHostel form that allows users to search for and select a location, displaying the selected address and capturing latitude/longitude coordinates

Implement form submission in the AddHostel page that calls the backend addHostel function with all collected data, displays success/error messages, and redirects to the newly created hostel detail page on success
Add a prominent 'Add Hostel' button in the Layout header navigation that links to the AddHostel form page

Add an Edit Hostel button to the HostelDetail page that navigates to an edit form page (/edit-hostel/:id) where users can modify the existing hostel's room sharing options and their corresponding prices

Create an EditHostel page component at frontend/src/pages/EditHostel.tsx that displays a form similar to AddHostel but pre-populated with existing hostel data, focusing specifically on editing the roomSharing array (room types and their prices)
Add a new route in frontend/src/App.tsx for /edit-hostel/:id that renders the EditHostel page component

Create a React Query mutation hook useUpdateHostel in frontend/src/hooks/useMutations.ts that calls the backend updateHostel function, invalidates the hostels query cache on success, and handles loading/error states

Update the EditHostel page component (frontend/src/pages/EditHostel.tsx) to include input fields for one-sharing, two-sharing, three-sharing, four-sharing, and five-sharing room types with their corresponding price inputs, allowing users to edit prices for all five sharing capacities

Create a Privacy page component at frontend/src/pages/Privacy.tsx that displays a comprehensive privacy policy covering data collection, usage, storage, user rights, and contact information, styled consistently with the existing warm terracotta/sage/amber color palette

Create a Disclaimer page component at frontend/src/pages/Disclaimer.tsx that displays legal disclaimers covering liability limitations, accuracy of information, third-party content, and terms of use, styled consistently with the existing design system
Add routes for /privacy and /disclaimer in frontend/src/App.tsx that render the Privacy and Disclaimer page components respectively
Add Privacy and Disclaimer links to the footer section of the Layout component (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) that navigate to the respective pages

Update frontend/index.html to include comprehensive SEO meta tags: a descriptive <title> tag ('Hostel Addas - Hostels in Hyderabad | Girls, Boys & Co-Living PG'), a <meta name='description'> with a 150-160 character description about hostel listings in Hyderabad, <meta name='keywords'> with relevant terms (hostels in Hyderabad, PG accommodation, girls hostel, boys hostel, co-living, paying guest), Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:type, og:url, og:site_name), and Twitter Card meta tags (twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description). Also add <meta name='robots' content='index, follow'> and a canonical link tag.

Create a static sitemap file at frontend/public/sitemap.xml listing the main routes of the app: home (/), privacy (/privacy), and disclaimer (/disclaimer). Include <lastmod>, <changefreq>, and <priority> elements for each URL. The domain should use a placeholder that matches the ICP canister URL pattern.

Create a robots.txt file at frontend/public/robots.txt that allows all search engine crawlers to index the site (User-agent: *, Disallow: nothing), and includes a Sitemap directive pointing to the sitemap.xml URL.

Add a visible 'How to get found on Google' informational banner or section on the Home page (frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx) that is dismissible. It should display a short message explaining that the site has been optimized for search engines and instructing the site owner to submit it to Google Search Console at search.google.com/search-console for faster indexing. Style it consistently with the warm terracotta/amber color palette. Store the dismissed state in localStorage so it does not reappear after being closed.

Create a Google site verification HTML file at frontend/public/googleXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX.html (using a placeholder filename that follows Google's standard verification file naming pattern, e.g. google-site-verification.html) that contains the standard Google verification meta tag content. Since the actual verification code must be provided by the user from Google Search Console, add a clearly named placeholder file at frontend/public/google-site-verification.html with instructional content explaining that the user must replace this file with the actual file downloaded from Google Search Console (https://search.google.com/search-console), which will have a filename like 'googleXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX.html' and content like 'google-site-verificationXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX'.

Add JSON-LD structured data (Schema.org markup) to frontend/index.html and key pages. Include a WebSite schema with SearchAction on the home page, and a LocalBusiness / LodgingBusiness schema for the site overall. Also add BreadcrumbList schema to the Privacy and Disclaimer pages. This helps Google understand the site content and display rich results.

Update the SeoInfoBanner component (frontend/src/components/SeoInfoBanner.tsx) to include a step-by-step 'How to find your site on Google' guide section. The guide should explain: (1) After submitting the sitemap to Google Search Console, indexing can take 1–4 weeks; (2) Users can check if their site is indexed by typing 'site:YOUR-CANISTER-URL' in Google search; (3) They can also search for 'Hostel Addas Hyderabad' or 'hostels in Hyderabad PG' to find the listing; (4) Share the site URL on social media and local directories to build backlinks and speed up indexing. Style this guide consistently with the existing warm terracotta/amber palette, and keep it dismissible per the existing localStorage logic.

Add a dedicated '/seo-guide' page component at frontend/src/pages/SeoGuide.tsx that provides a comprehensive, persistent SEO and Google visibility guide for the site owner. The page should cover: (1) How to verify the site is indexed using 'site:canister-url' search; (2) How to submit and monitor the sitemap in Google Search Console; (3) How to search for the site using branded terms ('Hostel Addas Hyderabad') and category terms ('girls hostel Hyderabad', 'boys PG Hyderabad', 'co-living Hyderabad'); (4) Tips for improving ranking: getting listed on JustDial, Sulekha, MagicBricks PG section and linking back to the site; (5) Expected timeline (1–4 weeks after Search Console submission for first appearance). Register this route in frontend/src/App.tsx and add a subtle 'SEO Guide' link in the footer of frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx.

Enhance the sitemap at frontend/public/sitemap.xml to include hostel detail page URL patterns and update <lastmod> dates to today's date (2025-07-10). Also add <changefreq>weekly</changefreq> for the home page to signal frequent content updates to crawlers.

Add additional location-specific and long-tail SEO keywords to the <meta name='keywords'> tag and the Open Graph description in frontend/index.html. Include terms such as: 'PG in Hyderabad', 'paying guest Hyderabad', 'affordable hostels Hyderabad', 'girls PG near Hitech City', 'boys hostel Kukatpally', 'co-living spaces Gachibowli', 'hostel near JNTU Hyderabad', 'student accommodation Hyderabad'. Also update the <meta name='description'> to weave in 2–3 of these high-value location terms naturally within the 150-160 character limit.

Generate static hostel image assets for the new Hyderabad hostel seed data. Create 6 images total: 2 for girls hostels (warm interior, clean room), 2 for boys hostels (modern dormitory, common area), and 2 for co-living spaces (shared lounge, well-lit bedroom). Save them in frontend/public/assets/generated/ and reference them in the backend seed data added in REQ-50.

Update the ImageGallery component (frontend/src/components/ImageGallery.tsx) and the HostelDetail page (frontend/src/pages/HostelDetail.tsx) to properly display all images associated with a hostel in a navigable gallery. Ensure that when a hostel has multiple images, the gallery shows all of them with previous/next navigation and clickable thumbnails. Fallback to a category-appropriate placeholder if a hostel has no images.

Ensure the HostelMap component (frontend/src/components/HostelMap.tsx) renders markers for all new Hyderabad hostels using their latitude/longitude coordinates from the backend. Each marker should show the hostel name and category on hover/click. The map view on the Home page should display all seeded hostels with correctly placed markers within the Hyderabad region.

Ensure the AddHostel page (frontend/src/pages/AddHostel.tsx) fully supports adding hostels with: name, category (Girls/Boys/Co-Living), description, full address, owner contact phone number, latitude/longitude via the MapLocationPicker, and individual price inputs for 1-sharing, 2-sharing, 3-sharing, 4-sharing, and 5-sharing room types. Verify the form submits all data to the backend addHostel function and redirects to the new hostel's detail page on success.

Ensure the EditHostel page (frontend/src/pages/EditHostel.tsx) is accessible from the HostelDetail page via the Edit Hostel button, pre-populates all 5 sharing type price fields (1-sharing through 5-sharing) with current hostel data, and saves updates via the backend updateHostel function. Show a success toast on save and return the user to the hostel detail page.

Display the owner contact number prominently on both the HostelCard component (frontend/src/components/HostelCard.tsx) and the HostelDetail page (frontend/src/pages/HostelDetail.tsx). On the card, show it below the address with a phone icon. On the detail page, show it in a dedicated contact section near the hostel name with a clickable tel: link for direct calling.

Fix the category filter functionality on the Home page (frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx) so that selecting 'All Hostels', 'Girls Only', 'Boys Only', or 'Co-Living' correctly filters the displayed hostel listings. Investigate whether the issue is in the CategoryFilter component state, the query hook (useGetHostelsByCategory vs useGetAllHostels), or the filtering logic applied to the hostel list, and ensure selecting each category tab shows only hostels matching that category (or all hostels for 'All').

Audit and fix the category filtering logic in frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx so that selecting 'Girls Only', 'Boys Only', 'Co-Living', or 'All Hostels' from the CategoryFilter component correctly filters and displays only the matching hostel listings. The fix must ensure: (1) the selected category value passed from CategoryFilter exactly matches the category strings stored in the backend ('Girls', 'Boys', 'Co-Living'); (2) the filtering comparison is case-insensitive or uses exact-match normalisation so no hostels are silently dropped; (3) selecting 'All Hostels' shows every hostel regardless of category; (4) the filtered results are applied after (not before) the search, price-range, and sharing-type filters so all filter types remain composable.

Fix the category filtering bug in frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx and frontend/src/components/CategoryFilter.tsx so that clicking 'Girls Only' correctly filters and displays only girls hostels. Trace the full data flow: (1) confirm the CategoryFilter component emits exactly 'Girls' (not 'Girls Only', 'girls', or any other variant) when the Girls Only tab is clicked; (2) ensure the filtering logic in Home.tsx compares each hostel's category field against the selected value using case-insensitive exact match or strict normalised comparison; (3) verify the backend seed data in backend/main.mo stores the category as 'Girls' (matching capitalisation) and the getHostelsByCategory function uses exact equality; (4) ensure selecting 'All Hostels' resets the filter and shows all hostels; (5) confirm the same fix applies consistently to 'Boys Only' (emitting 'Boys') and 'Co-Living' (emitting 'Co-Living') so all category tabs work correctly.

Create a dedicated '/download' page component at frontend/src/pages/Download.tsx that presents a mobile app download section for Hostel Addas. The page should explain that the site is available as a Progressive Web App (PWA) installable directly from the browser on both Android and iOS. Include clear step-by-step installation instructions for Android (Chrome: tap the three-dot menu → 'Add to Home Screen') and iOS (Safari: tap the Share icon → 'Add to Home Screen'). Display app feature highlights (hostel search, category filters, maps, contact owners). Style the page consistently with the existing warm terracotta/sage/amber color palette. Include a prominent app icon/logo visual and QR code placeholder section for sharing the site URL.
Register the '/download' route in frontend/src/App.tsx that renders the Download page component, nested under the existing Layout root route alongside the other page routes.

Add a 'Download App' link to the footer section of the Layout component (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) and a prominent 'Get the App' button in the sticky header navigation, both linking to the '/download' page. The button in the header should be styled similarly to the existing 'Add Hostel' button but use a distinct accent color within the warm palette to differentiate it.

Add PWA-enabling meta tags and a Web App Manifest to support 'Add to Home Screen' installation. Create frontend/public/manifest.json with name 'Hostel Addas', short_name 'Hostel Addas', start_url '/', display 'standalone', background_color matching the warm terracotta palette (#C1440E or closest token), theme_color matching the same, and icons referencing existing assets. Link the manifest in frontend/index.html via <link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json'>. Ensure the existing apple-mobile-web-app meta tags and theme-color meta tag in index.html are present and correctly set.

Create a reusable AdBanner component at frontend/src/components/AdBanner.tsx that renders a styled advertisement placeholder banner. The component should accept props for size ('leaderboard' 728x90, 'rectangle' 300x250, 'banner' 468x60), a label ('Advertisement'), and an optional ad slot ID or image URL. Style it with a light warm-toned background (amber/cream tones from the existing palette), a subtle border, and centered 'Advertisement' label text. Include a note in the component that ad network script tags (e.g. Google AdSense) can be injected here once an account is approved.

Place AdBanner components in high-visibility positions on the Home page (frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx): (1) a leaderboard banner (728x90) below the search/filter controls and above the hostel grid on desktop, collapsing to a rectangle (300x250) on mobile; (2) a rectangle banner (300x250) at the bottom of the hostel grid, after every 6 hostel cards (inline between cards in the grid). Ensure ad placements do not disrupt the hostel listing layout and are clearly labelled 'Advertisement'.

Place an AdBanner rectangle (300x250) on the HostelDetail page (frontend/src/pages/HostelDetail.tsx) in the sidebar area on desktop (beside the room sharing/pricing section) and below the contact section on mobile. The ad should be clearly labelled 'Advertisement' and styled consistently with the page.

Add a 'Sponsored' badge and priority placement feature to hostel listings. In the backend (backend/main.mo), add an optional boolean field `isSponsored` to the Hostel type (defaulting to false). Update the addHostel and updateHostel functions to accept and store this field. In the frontend HostelCard component (frontend/src/components/HostelCard.tsx), display a 'Sponsored' badge (amber/gold colored pill label) in the top-left corner of the card image when `isSponsored` is true. In the Home page (frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx), sort sponsored hostels to appear before non-sponsored hostels in the listing, regardless of other sort order.

Create an 'Advertise With Us' page at frontend/src/pages/Advertise.tsx that explains the monetization and advertising opportunities available on Hostel Addas. The page should include: (1) a hero section with headline 'Grow Your Business with Hostel Addas'; (2) a 'Sponsored Listing' section explaining that hostel owners can pay to have their listing shown at the top with a Sponsored badge; (3) an 'Ad Placements' section explaining banner ad slots available on the home and detail pages with approximate visitor reach; (4) a 'Visitor Stats' section with a placeholder showing site traffic metrics (e.g. 'Growing audience of students and working professionals in Hyderabad'); (5) a contact/inquiry section with a WhatsApp or email call-to-action (use a placeholder number/email with instruction to update). Style consistently with the warm terracotta/sage/amber palette.
Register the '/advertise' route in frontend/src/App.tsx that renders the Advertise page component, nested under the existing Layout root route alongside other page routes.

Add an 'Advertise' link to the footer section of the Layout component (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) linking to the '/advertise' page. Also add a small 'Advertise' text link in the sticky header navigation (beside the existing nav links) so site visitors and hostel owners can easily discover the monetization page.

Add a simple visitor counter feature to the backend (backend/main.mo): add a stable `var visitorCount : Nat = 0` variable and a public function `recordVisit() : async Nat` that increments the counter and returns the new total, plus a query function `getVisitorCount() : async Nat` that returns the current count. On the frontend, call `recordVisit()` once per session (guard with sessionStorage to avoid double-counting on re-renders) in the Home page (frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx) when the page mounts. Display the total visitor count in the footer of the Layout component (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) as a small stat: 'Visitors: X' styled subtly in the footer text.

Create a 'Monetization & Earnings' dashboard page at frontend/src/pages/Earnings.tsx that gives the site owner a clear overview of all active revenue streams: (1) a 'Display Ads' section explaining how to activate Google AdSense — step-by-step instructions to sign up at adsense.google.com, get the site approved, paste the AdSense script tag into the AdBanner component's designated injection comment, and replace the placeholder `data-ad-slot` values with real slot IDs; (2) a 'Sponsored Listings' section showing how hostel owners pay to have their listing featured at the top with the amber 'Sponsored' badge, with a contact CTA (WhatsApp/email placeholder with instructions to update); (3) a 'Visitor Stats' section displaying the live visitor count fetched from the backend `getVisitorCount` query (already implemented), styled as a prominent stat card; (4) a 'Potential Earnings Estimator' section with a simple interactive calculator — user inputs estimated monthly visitors and an average CPM (cost per thousand impressions), and the page calculates an estimated monthly ad revenue figure. Style the page consistently with the warm terracotta/sage/amber palette.
Register the '/earnings' route in frontend/src/App.tsx that renders the Earnings page component, nested under the existing Layout root route alongside other page routes.

Add an 'Earnings' link to the footer section of the Layout component (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) linking to the '/earnings' page, so the site owner can easily access the monetization dashboard. Also add a subtle 'Earn' or 'Monetize' text link in the sticky header navigation beside the existing 'Advertise' link.

Update the AdBanner component (frontend/src/components/AdBanner.tsx) to include a clearly commented placeholder block inside the component's render output where Google AdSense `<ins class='adsbygoogle'>` tags and the corresponding `adsbygoogle.push({})` script can be pasted by the site owner once their AdSense account is approved. The placeholder block should be hidden visually (rendered as an empty fragment by default) but contain a detailed HTML comment explaining: (1) delete the placeholder div and uncomment the AdSense block; (2) replace `data-ad-client` with the publisher ID (ca-pub-XXXXXXXX); (3) replace `data-ad-slot` with the specific ad unit slot ID. The visible placeholder banner UI (amber gradient, 'Advertisement' label) should remain unchanged for display until real ads are activated.

Update the HostelCard component (frontend/src/components/HostelCard.tsx) to display a compact row of amenity icons/pills below the price and sharing details. Show up to 4–5 amenity tags (e.g. WiFi, AC, Meals) as small badge-style pills using the warm amber/sage color palette. If there are more amenities than shown, display a '+N more' indicator. Only display the amenities row when the hostel has at least one amenity.

Update the HostelDetail page (frontend/src/pages/HostelDetail.tsx) to display a dedicated 'Amenities' section that lists all amenities for the hostel. Each amenity should be shown as an icon+label pill or badge. Use appropriate icons (e.g. WiFi icon for WiFi, snowflake for AC, fork-and-knife for Meals, car for Parking, dumbbell for Gym). Style the section consistently with the existing warm terracotta/sage/amber color palette, placed between the room sharing/pricing section and the map section.

Update the EditHostel page (frontend/src/pages/EditHostel.tsx) to include an amenities editing section. Display a checklist or multi-select input of common amenities (WiFi, AC, Laundry, Parking, Meals, Gym, Hot Water, Power Backup, Security, CCTV, Study Room, Common Kitchen). Pre-populate the checkboxes with the hostel's currently stored amenities. Allow the user to check/uncheck amenities. Include the updated amenities array in the form submission payload sent to the backend `updateHostel` function.

Update the mutation hook in frontend/src/hooks/useMutations.ts so that the `useUpdateHostel` mutation passes the amenities field (alongside the existing roomSharing data) to the backend `updateHostel` function call.

new_feature_requests.summary: Display hostels list with categories (girls, boys, co-living)
Show hostel images for each listing
Integrate maps for hostel locations
Display room sharing details and pricing
Create clean and neat homepage
Create a backend data model for hostels that includes: hostel name, category (girls/boys/co-living), description, address, price per room, room sharing capacity, and image URLs
Implement backend functions to add, retrieve, and list hostels with filtering by category (girls/boys/co-living)
Add sample hostel data for Hyderabad including girls, boys, and co-living hostels with sharing-based pricing

Add sample hostel data for Hyderabad including girls hostels, boys hostels, and co-living hostels with detailed information: hostel name, category, address in Hyderabad, description, room sharing capacity (single, double, triple, four-sharing), and price per bed based on sharing arrangement
Add hostel owner contact number to hostel listings and detail views
Add an ownerContact field (Text type) to the Hostel data type in backend/main.mo to store the hostel owner's phone number
Add search bar to find hostels by name or location
Add filter functionality for hostel listings (by category, price range, sharing capacity)
Add sort functionality for hostel listings (by price, name, location)
Add manual entry form for creating new hostels with all details including Google Maps integration

Create a backend function addHostel that accepts hostel details (name, category, description, address, ownerContact, roomSharing options with prices, and coordinates) and stores a new hostel in the hostels HashMap, returning the generated hostel ID
Add editing functionality for room types and prices in existing hostel entries

Create a backend function updateHostel in backend/main.mo that accepts a hostel ID and updated roomSharing data, validates the hostel exists, updates only the roomSharing field in the hostels HashMap, and returns a success/error result

Ensure the backend updateHostel function in backend/main.mo accepts and properly updates roomSharing arrays that include one-sharing, two-sharing, three-sharing, four-sharing, and five-sharing entries with their respective prices
Add privacy policy page to the website
Add disclaimer page to the website
Add SEO meta tags (title, description, keywords, Open Graph) to improve Google search visibility and indexing
Upload Google site verification file to the website for Google Search Console verification

Improve SEO further to increase Google search visibility - add structured data (JSON-LD schema markup), improve page titles, add more location-specific keywords, and provide user guidance on how to search for the site on Google
Create a mobile app version of the hostel listing site

Update the Layout component (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) to use a mobile-first responsive design: replace the desktop sticky header with a top app bar that shows only the logo/site name on mobile, add a bottom navigation bar on mobile screens with tabs for Home, Map, Add Hostel, and a hamburger menu or drawer for additional links (Privacy, Disclaimer, SEO Guide). The bottom nav should use the existing warm terracotta/amber color palette and highlight the active tab.

Update frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx to use a mobile-optimized layout: stack the search bar, category filter, advanced filters, and sort controls vertically in a single-column layout on mobile. The category filter should render as a horizontally scrollable pill row. The hostel grid should switch to a single-column card list on mobile. The map view toggle should be accessible via a floating action button (FAB) on mobile rather than an inline toggle.

Update the HostelCard component (frontend/src/components/HostelCard.tsx) to be touch-friendly for mobile: increase tap target sizes (minimum 44px height for interactive elements), use a full-width card layout on mobile with the image displayed as a banner at the top (aspect ratio 16:9), and show the hostel name, category badge, price, and contact number in a compact summary below the image. Add a subtle tap/press visual feedback using CSS active state.

Update the HostelDetail page (frontend/src/pages/HostelDetail.tsx) for mobile: make the image gallery full-width with swipe-friendly navigation (use CSS scroll-snap on a horizontally scrollable strip), stack all content sections (room sharing, contact, map, description) vertically, and display a sticky bottom bar on mobile with the owner contact number and a 'Call Now' tel: link button styled in the warm color palette.

Update the AddHostel page (frontend/src/pages/AddHostel.tsx) and EditHostel page (frontend/src/pages/EditHostel.tsx) for mobile: each form step should occupy the full viewport height with vertically stacked fields, increase input height to at least 48px for touch usability, and make the step navigation buttons (Previous/Next/Submit) full-width on mobile. The Google Maps location picker should render at a height of at least 300px on mobile.

Add a viewport meta tag and mobile web app meta tags to frontend/index.html to enable a native app-like experience: ensure `<meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover'>` is present, add `<meta name='mobile-web-app-capable' content='yes'>`, `<meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-capable' content='yes'>`, `<meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style' content='default'>`, and `<meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-title' content='Hostel Addas'>`. Also add a `<meta name='theme-color'>` using the primary terracotta color (#C1440E or closest token).

Update frontend/tailwind.config.js and frontend/src/index.css to ensure all touch interaction states are properly styled: add `touch-action: manipulation` to buttons and links to eliminate the 300ms tap delay, ensure focus-visible outlines are preserved for accessibility, and add safe-area-inset padding support (env(safe-area-inset-bottom)) to the bottom navigation bar and sticky footer elements so content is not obscured by device notches or home indicators.

Expand the backend seed data in backend/main.mo to include at least 9 real Hyderabad hostels (3 Girls, 3 Boys, 3 Co-Living). Each hostel must have: name, category, full Hyderabad address (e.g. Kukatpally, Gachibowli, JNTU, Hitech City, Madhapur, Ameerpet areas), description, ownerContact phone number, latitude/longitude coordinates for Hyderabad, and a roomSharing array covering 1-sharing, 2-sharing, 3-sharing, 4-sharing, and 5-sharing with realistic per-bed prices in INR (ranging roughly ₹4000–₹12000 depending on sharing type and category). Assign at least 2 image URLs per hostel using the existing static asset paths under /assets/generated/.

Verify and fix the backend category filtering logic in backend/main.mo so that the getHostelsByCategory function (or equivalent) correctly matches and returns hostels whose category field equals the requested category string (e.g. 'Girls', 'Boys', 'Co-Living'). Ensure the category values stored in the seed data and returned by the backend exactly match the values expected by the frontend filter.

Verify that the category values stored in the backend seed data in backend/main.mo exactly match the strings expected by the frontend filter ('Girls', 'Boys', 'Co-Living') — including capitalisation and spacing. If any seed hostel has a mismatched category value (e.g. 'girls', 'GIRLS', 'Co-living'), correct it so all category strings are consistent. Also confirm the getHostelsByCategory backend function compares category strings using exact equality against these normalised values.
Add a mobile app download section/page to the website so users can download or install the mobile app
Add advertisement placements and visitor-based earning features to the site (ad banners, sponsored listings, or similar monetization)

Add a backend query function `getEarningsStats() : async { visitorCount: Nat }` to backend/main.mo that returns current visitor count (reusing the existing `visitorCount` stable variable). This gives the Earnings dashboard a dedicated stats endpoint that can be extended in future without breaking the existing `getVisitorCount` function.
Add amenities field to each hostel (e.g. WiFi, AC, laundry, parking, meals, gym, etc.) and display them on hostel listing cards and detail pages
Add ability to edit hostel amenities from the EditHostel page or hostel detail page
